---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

I recommend installing Zabbix agent 2.

For a standard monitoring practice on a modern OS like Windows 10, Agent 2 is the better choice. While both will collect essential metrics like CPU, memory, and disk usage—similar to how you used the Windows Exporter in your Prometheus practice—Zabbix Agent 2 is the newer, more efficient version. It offers better performance and easier management for most modern monitoring scenarios.

During the Zabbix Agent 2 installation process on your Windows 10 VM, you will see a configuration screen that looks like the image below. You must input your Zabbix Server's fixed IP address (which should be 10.0.2.12 based on your prior monitoring setup) in both the "Zabbix server IP or DNS name (active)" and "Zabbix server IP or DNS name (passive)" fields.

Doing this will ensure the Agent installed on your Windows machine (10.0.2.10) can communicate with and send monitoring data—like CPU and RAM utilization—to your central Zabbix Server. The following image simulates this configuration step.
